title: Gujarati Handvo

subs.name: replace half the rava with rice flour for a crispier base
equal quantity buttermilk; reduce water to zero
150 g grated zucchini or ridge gourd, squeezed dry
0.75 tsp Eno fruit salt for a lighter, spongier texture

steps.text: In a large bowl whisk together rava, besan, curd and water until smooth. Rest the batter for 15 minutes so the rava absorbs the curd and softens slightly. Meanwhile squeeze the grated bottle gourd in your palms to remove excess water — this prevents the handvo from becoming soggy.

Add the squeezed bottle gourd, grated carrot, peas, green chilli, ginger, turmeric, red chilli powder, sugar and salt to the rested batter. Mix well. Just before cooking add baking soda and fold it in — the batter will bubble lightly.

Heat 2 tbsp oil in a thick-bottomed kadai (25 cm diameter works well) on medium. Add mustard seeds; once they pop add sesame seeds and curry leaves and fry for 10 seconds. The tempering goes straight on the base of the pan.

Immediately pour the entire batter over the tempering in the kadai. Spread it gently with a spatula to an even disc about 2.5 cm thick. Drizzle 1 tbsp oil around the edges. Cover tightly with a lid and cook on low-medium flame for 15 minutes.

Lift the lid after 15 minutes — the top should look dry and set (no wet batter visible) and the sides should have pulled away slightly from the kadai. Drizzle the remaining 1 tbsp oil around the edges and gently slide a flat spatula under the handvo to loosen it. Invert it onto a large plate and then carefully slide it back into the kadai with the cooked side up. Cover and cook for a further 8–10 minutes until the second side is golden and the cake is cooked through.
Slide the handvo onto a cutting board, let it rest for 5 minutes, then cut into wedges. Serve warm with green coriander-mint chutney. Leftovers reheat well in a pan with a few drops of oil.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Fermentation conditions affect safety. Discard batches with unexpected mould, putrid odour or other spoilage signs.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: bottle-gourd → 150 g grated zucchini or ridge gourd, squeezed dry (any mild watery gourd works); rava → replace half the rava with rice flour for a crispier base; curd → equal quantity buttermilk; reduce water to zero; baking-soda → 0.75 tsp Eno fruit salt for a lighter, spongier texture
